fear: That Benezette will discover the approach from the Chicago utility agent and understand immediately what Edgar has been considering.
name: Edgar Williams
role: Partner, Williams and Williams; project business manager
voice: Warm, slightly too smooth — the voice of a man who has learned to make disagreement sound like agreement until the moment it matters.
secrets: He has already accepted $200 from Harlan Stede as a retainer against the bond transaction — the money is in his coat lining.
He is the one who told Stede the exact depth at which the drill was currently working, information that only someone with access to the daily bore log would have.
knowledge: He received the approach from Chicago utility agent Harlan Stede six days ago and has not refused it.
He knows the falsified cost certificate exists because he was shown a draft of it by Alderman Voss — he did not create it, but he did not report it.
He has the signed original of the special assessment roll locked in his satchel, which would immediately disprove the falsified figures if produced.
publicFace: Benezette's younger brother and financial partner — affable, well-dressed for a construction site, and always carrying a leather document satchel. He handles the bond paperwork so Benezette doesn't have to.
trustLogic: Responds well to appeals to family loyalty and to any suggestion that Benezette's reputation is at stake. Shuts down when financial specifics are requested directly.
privateGoal: To quietly sell the remaining bond tranche to the Chicago utility agent before the project completes, privately benefiting from a potential financial arrangement tied to the bond issue without Benezette ever knowing the source.

aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Walks briskly toward the CBQ rail line, satchel under his arm, claiming he has a late train to catch.
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: Does not run — walks fast and talks faster, insisting there has been a misunderstanding and that he will return in the morning to sort it out.
aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Laughs, deflects with a fraternal anecdote about Benezette, and changes the subject to the tower's architectural merits.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will never voluntarily admit the $200 retainer or the information leak to Stede. He will confess the rest if cornered with the signed assessment roll.
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If the Night Marshal produces a sworn complaint or if Benezette directly accuses him in front of Wickerson.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Produces the document satchel and begins shuffling papers, buying time, insisting that everything is in order and that he would be happy to clarify once he has had a chance to review.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Sits down heavily, sets the satchel on his knees, and begins negotiating — offering the signed assessment roll in exchange for the Night Marshal not filing the sworn complaint until morning.
